Package de.uka.ilkd.key.logic

Class Semisequent

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.Semisequent
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<SequentFormula>
public class Semisequent extends Object implements Iterable<SequentFormula>
This class represents the succedent or antecendent part of a sequent. It is more or less a list without duplicates and subsumed formulas. This is ensured using method removeRedundancy. In future versions it can be enhanced to do other simplifications. A sequent and so a semisequent has to be immutable.

  • Field Details

    • EMPTY_SEMISEQUENT

      public static final Semisequent EMPTY_SEMISEQUENT
      the empty semisequent (using singleton pattern)

  • Constructor Details

    • Semisequent

      public Semisequent(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> seqList)
      Create a new Semisequent from an ordered collection of formulas. The provided list must be redundancy free, i.e., the created sequent must be exactly the same as when creating the sequent by subsequently inserting all formulas
      Parameters:
      seqList - list of sequent formulas

    • Semisequent

      public Semisequent(Collection<SequentFormula> seqList)
      Create a new Semisequent from an ordered collection of formulas. The provided collection must be redundancy free, i.e., the created sequent must be exactly the same as when creating the sequent by subsequently inserting all formulas
      Parameters:
      seqList - list of sequent formulas

    • Semisequent

      public Semisequent(SequentFormula seqFormula)
      creates a new Semisequent with the Semisequent elements in seqList

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static Semisequent create(Collection<SequentFormula> seqList)
      Create a new Semisequent from an ordered collection of formulas (possibly empty). The provided collection must be redundancy free, i.e., the created sequent must be exactly the same as when creating the sequent by subsequently inserting all formulas.
      Parameters:
      seqList - list of sequent formulas

    • insert

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insert(int idx, SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      inserts an element at a specified index performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      idx - int encoding the place the element has to be put
      sequentFormula - SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • insert

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insert(int idx, ImmutableList<SequentFormula> insertionList)
      inserts the elements of the list at the specified index performing redundancy checks
      Parameters:
      idx - int encoding the place where the insertion starts
      insertionList - IList to be inserted starting at idx
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • insertFirst

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insertFirst(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      inserts element at index 0 performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • insertFirst

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insertFirst(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> insertions)
      inserts element at index 0 performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      insertions - IList to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • insertLast

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insertLast(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      inserts element at the end of the semisequent performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • insertLast

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insertLast(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> insertions)
      inserts the formulas of the list at the end of the semisequent performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      insertions - the IList to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      is this a semisequent that contains no formulas
      Returns:
      true if the semisequent contains no formulas

    • replace

      public SemisequentChangeInfo replace(PosInOccurrence pos, SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      replaces the element at place idx with sequentFormula
      Parameters:
      pos - the PosInOccurrence describing the position of and within the formula below which the formula differs from the new formula sequentFormula
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula replacing the old element at index idx
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • replace

      public SemisequentChangeInfo replace(int idx, SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      replaces the idx-th formula by sequentFormula
      Parameters:
      idx - the int with the position of the formula to be replaced
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula replacing the formula at the given position
      Returns:
      a SemisequentChangeInfo containing the new sequent and a diff to the old one

    • replace

      public SemisequentChangeInfo replace(PosInOccurrence pos, ImmutableList<SequentFormula> replacements)
      replaces the element at place idx with the first element of the given list and adds the rest of the list to the semisequent behind the replaced formula
      Parameters:
      pos - the formula to be replaced
      replacements - the IList whose head replaces the element at index idx and the tail is added to the semisequent
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • replace

      public SemisequentChangeInfo replace(int idx, ImmutableList<SequentFormula> replacements)
      replaces the formula at position idx by the given list of formulas
      Parameters:
      idx - the position
      replacements - the new formulas
      Returns:
      change information including the resulting semisequent after the replacement

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns:
      int counting the elements of this semisequent

    • remove

      public SemisequentChangeInfo remove(int idx)
      removes an element
      Parameters:
      idx - int being the index of the element that has to be removed
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • indexOf

      public int indexOf(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      returns the index of the given SequentFormula or -1 if the sequent formula is not found. Equality of sequent formulas is checked sing the identy check (i.e., ==)
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula to look for
      Returns:
      index of sequentFormula (-1 if not found)

    • get

      public SequentFormula get(int idx)
      gets the element at a specific index
      Parameters:
      idx - int representing the index of the element we want to have
      Returns:
      SequentFormula found at index idx
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if idx is negative or greater or equal to Sequent.size()

    • getFirst

      public SequentFormula getFirst()
      Returns:
      the first SequentFormula of this Semisequent

    • contains

      public boolean contains(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      checks if the SequentFormula occurs in this Semisequent (identity check)
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula to look for
      Returns:
      true iff. sequentFormula has been found in this Semisequent

    • containsEqual

      public boolean containsEqual(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      checks if a SequentFormula is in this Semisequent (equality check)
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula to look for
      Returns:
      true iff. sequentFormula has been found in this Semisequent

    • iterator

      public Iterator<SequentFormula> iterator()
      returns iterator about the elements of the sequent
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<SequentFormula>
      Returns:
      Iterator
